rule: Only PROVEN couplings. Each cites file:line for BOTH ends of the dependency. A coupling with one end cited is a hypothesis and was dropped.

retracted_from_first_pass.was: os.environ is per-process; a Studio go_live cannot reach a resident daemon
retracted_from_first_pass.why: cli.py:1303-1304 — the daemon loop calls load_dotenv(cfg.root/'.env', override=True) AND rebuilds Config(cfg.root) on EVERY TICK ('operator disk truth each tick'). A go_live .env write DOES reach the resident daemon within one tick.

couplings.title: Two lock domains that do not exclude each other — PROVEN silent data loss

ends.end: SQLite BEGIN IMMEDIATE — every ledger writer
os.replace on the db path + unlink of -wal/-shm

couplings.executed_experiment: A live writer holding BEGIN IMMEDIATE had restore_snapshot swap the database file out from under it. The writer's commit() SUCCEEDED — no exception raised — and committed to the now-orphaned inode. Its write was SILENTLY DISCARDED; the final on-disk state was the snapshot's.
couplings.exposure: restore_snapshot has NO production caller (operator break-glass), but ledger_wipe.py:246 advertises it as THE wipe rollback path.

couplings.title: A running Studio never re-reads .env; the daemon does

ends.end: daemon loop: load_dotenv(override=True) + Config rebuild EVERY tick
Studio: load_dotenv ONCE at process entry, then blocks in app.run

couplings.consequence: A .env change made by the CLI or the daemon NEVER reaches a running Studio. golive.py:11 acknowledges this outright — it is precisely why _dual_write also pokes os.environ (golive.py:66), so the WRITING Studio process reflects its own change immediately. A Studio that did not perform the write shows stale env until restarted.
couplings.direction: OPPOSITE to what Cycle-2's first pass claimed

couplings.title: Post.error_reason is a structured control channel, not a message
couplings.consequence: Three independent parsers read machine state out of one free-text field written by ~14 sites. Any writer that overwrites error_reason with free text silently RESETS the retry budget and CLEARS the give-up marker. reconcile.py:714 does exactly this deliberately on a successful publish.

couplings.title: Magic filename shape couples ingest's id-minting to rebuild's orphan detection

ends.end: the id format it encodes: make_id('src', sha) = 'src_' + sha1[:12]
orphan detection consumes the filename shape

couplings.consequence: Changing the id length or prefix in ids.py silently breaks orphan detection in ledger.py — rebuild_catalog would stop seeing real orphans, with no error.

couplings.title: _VALID_BACKENDS is defined twice; the two homes serve different boundaries

ends.end: config.py:72 — used by Config.poster_backend's READ-time validation
settings.py:18 — used by accounts.set_backend's WRITE-time validation

couplings.consequence: If they drift, the write boundary and the read boundary disagree about what a legal backend is — the exact class of gap INV-03 exploits. accounts.py imports _VALID_BACKENDS from settings while importing _LIVE_BACKENDS/_BACKEND_PLATFORMS/FRAMING_NAMES from config on the ADJACENT line (accounts.py:12-13).

couplings.title: The gate-kind registry is triplicated and unlinked; adding a kind needs 3 edits
couplings.consequence: intro_match is the PROOF this coupling is live and unguarded: it is registered in registry #1 only, and is therefore permanently unanswerable. Nothing — no test, no type, no assertion — ties the three registries together.

couplings.title: actions_casting is safe ONLY because Moment.validate_assignment=True

ends.end: cast_add/cast_remove mutate affinities via direct setattr
Moment.model_config validate_assignment=True (the ONLY model with it)
the consumer that requires canonical handles: affinity_admits (the crosspost gate)

couplings.consequence: PROVEN by execution: model_copy bypasses Moment's validators DESPITE validate_assignment=True. Converting cast_add to model_copy 'for consistency with the rest of the codebase' would silently stop canonicalizing affinities — and a non-canonical '@Foo' would never match surf.account == 'foo', so the moment would post NOWHERE, silently.

couplings.title: The pipe-delimited composite-key format is an unenforced schema
couplings.consequence: Three maps key on hand-built pipe-delimited strings with no shared constructor. An account handle containing '|' would corrupt all three. The handle charset — defined in a DIFFERENT module — is load-bearing to the ledger key space.

couplings.title: Nine lazy in-function imports exist solely to break module cycles

ends.end: accounts -> ledger (one-way; ledger never imports accounts)
ledger -> timeutil (kept stdlib-only to stay cycle-safe)

couplings.consequence: Module initialization order is load-bearing. Hoisting any one of these to a top-level import breaks the process at start.

couplings.title: providers.py is a structural false-dead-code source

ends.end: dispatched from a dict, never by name

couplings.consequence: A name-based call graph flags all six as 'zero callers'. All six are live. Same trap: post.compress.persist_post_shrink (lazy-imported at studio/actions.py:406).

couplings.title: _BACKEND_PLATFORMS hardcodes deployment topology inside config
couplings.consequence: A deployment where Postiz also serves TikTok would silently refuse to bridge that channel. The config constant, not the account registry, decides.

couplings.title: PLATFORM_MAX_SECONDS fails open BY OMISSION

ends.end: the cap dict — a platform ABSENT from it has no clamp

couplings.consequence: Omission is a SILENT policy decision, indistinguishable from an oversight. The comment records that the v1 version of this dict was 'declared but never enforced' — a FALSE safety contract that let a 180s pick fan out over YouTube's cap.

couplings.title: LIFT_SCORE is one literal with many readers; a misspelled read is a silent zero

ends.end: read by adjust / digest / variant_* / studio
ends.cite: models.py:134-137 (the comment states the failure mode)

couplings.consequence: A key typo at any read site makes that scorer silently treat every post as 'no lift data' — indistinguishable from 'not enough data yet'. Silent-degradation coupling.

couplings.title: 01_thirdparty_inbox is a deliberate directory-naming coupling

ends.end: declared a PEER of 01_inbox, not a child, to sit OUTSIDE the native ingest rglob
the consumer of the origin_kind it protects

couplings.consequence: Moving it under 01_inbox would silently relabel every third-party asset as native, corrupting origin_kind. The directory layout IS the type system here.

couplings.title: The Studio's path-traversal guard depends on render_path's construction, tied only by a comment

ends.end: Config.render_path — 'ALWAYS under self.base, so the Studio _bounded serve check passes'
the media-serving routes that rely on it

couplings.consequence: The render-path builder and the serve guard must agree, and only a comment ties them together.

couplings.title: A SECOND provider resolver that never checks cfg.is_live

ends.end: compress.publish_backend_for_post — effective_provider() or cfg.poster_backend or 'dryrun', with NO is_live check
the canonical resolver that DOES gate on is_live (GATE-1)

couplings.consequence: On a NOT-live system this returns a live backend name (e.g. 'zernio') where _post_provider would return 'dryrun'. It is NOT a publish bypass — its three consumers (upload_cap_bytes, apply_shrink_to_post, media_path_for_post at actions.py:350, actions.py:1099, views_results.py:141) only decide whether to SHRINK a file. The consequence is a wasted ffmpeg re-encode on a dryrun system, not a publish. Recorded because it is a divergent second answer to 'which backend publishes this post'.

couplings.title: Media-cache validity keys on a magic character in the URL

ends.end: _media_cache_hit: a Postiz URL is IDENTIFIED by containing a literal '|'
…and a Zernio URL is REJECTED if it contains one
an UNKNOWN backend falls through to a bare startswith('http')

couplings.consequence: Cache-reuse correctness across backends rests on an undocumented URL-shape convention. A Postiz URL format change that drops the '|' would silently invalidate every cached upload and re-upload every clip.
